feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Leading oncologist reveals 6 habits that could promote longevity and reduce your risk of chronic illness
entry.summary: Dr. Ezekiel Emanuel says dinner parties combine healthy food, mental activity, social life and exercise into one simple longevity strategy.

entry.title: New Alzheimer's blood test predicts who is likely to develop dementia in 5 to 10 years
entry.summary: A p-tau217 blood test predicted a 78% chance of cognitive decline within 10 years for symptom-free adults with very high levels of the biomarker.

entry.title: Cyclospora cases prompt questions about produce safety; Utah cases not linked to national outbreak
entry.summary: A growing Cyclospora outbreak across parts of the country is causing concern among consumers and raising questions about whether fresh produce is safe to eat.

entry.title: Safety fears limit Ebola response in Congo, with more than 12 attacks recorded
entry.summary: Authorities report at least a dozen attacks on health facilities and workers during Congo's Ebola outbreak. Safety fears are restricting the response in Ituri province. Many unpaid health and frontline workers have gone on strike, complicating efforts. This o…

entry.title: 7-Day High-Protein, High-Fiber Meal Plan for Beginners, Created by a Dietitian
entry.summary: This simple high-protein, high-fiber meal plan created by a dietitian features recipes that require no more than 15-minutes of active cooking time.

entry.title: 1 dead in Upper East Side Legionnaires' outbreak
entry.summary: The Health Department announced the first death in the ongoing outbreak linked to dozens of water-cooling towers.

entry.title: New study sheds light on how Epstein-Barr virus may contribute to multiple sclerosis
entry.summary: Multiple sclerosis (MS) is a chronic autoimmune disorder that attacks the central nervous system. For years, Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) has been strongly linked to MS, as nearly every person diagnosed with the condition shows evidence of past EBV infection. For…

entry.title: My eye had to be sewn shut after a parasite ate my cornea — the crucial mistake I made
entry.summary: “I’ve had three kids and giving birth is a dream compared to this pain,” Emma Marsden said.

entry.title: Could That Museum Visit Help You Live Longer?
entry.summary: A psychiatrist says engaging with the arts not only helps biologically. It can also make you feel more alive.

entry.title: Popular sugar substitutes linked to faster brain aging
entry.summary: Several popular sugar substitutes may not be as harmless as they seem. Adults who consumed the most artificial sweeteners showed substantially faster declines in memory and thinking, especially if they were under 60 or had diabetes. The highest intake was lin…

entry.title: Weight-loss drugs may improve job prospects and dating odds for one group
entry.summary: A weight-loss specialist says GLP-1 results boost confidence in job interviews and dating, but the study found no clear improvement in well-being.

entry.title: Elton John’s Go-To Sandwich Is a 3-Ingredient Classic
entry.summary: Elton John shares his favorite go-to comfort food, which is a simple sandwich made with bacon, butter and bread.

entry.title: Intermittent fasting helped people keep weight off for a year
entry.summary: A 12-week intermittent fasting program produced weight-loss benefits that were still visible a year later. Participants who ate within an eight-hour window maintained more weight loss than those who followed their usual longer eating schedule. Both early and …

entry.title: 8 Foods You Should Be Eating Every Week If You Have High Blood Pressure, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: If you have high blood pressure, what you eat can affect it. Discover eight foods to eat every week to help manage your blood pressure.

entry.title: So THAT'S Why Mosquitoes Bite Some People More Than Others
entry.summary: Here's what makes you so attractive to the bugs and how you can prevent bites.

entry.title: Should You Ever Be Naked Around Your Kids? Here's What Experts Actually Think
entry.summary: "If they are grossed out, scream ‘awkward’ in embarrassment or run, you have your answer."

entry.title: Doctors Say This 'Embarrassing' Symptom Could Be A Warning Sign Of Colorectal Cancer
entry.summary: It can indicate a more advanced stage of the disease or can be a sign of a different medical condition.

entry.title: It was a small scratch. But tetanus left her fighting for her life.
entry.summary: Tetanus is rare, but cases continue to occur in people who are unvaccinated or have gone more than 10 years between Td booster doses.

entry.title: What is adenomyosis? Pittsburgh area women, doctors aim to spread awareness
entry.summary: When Andrea Murray decided to undergo a hysterectomy after debilitating menstrual pain that began when she was around 15 years old, her life drastically changed for the better. “I would have abnormally extreme pain to the point where something was wrong…

entry.title: Advocates push to make alpha gal the 10th major food allergen
entry.summary: According to a 2023 Centers for Disease Control and Prevention estimate, almost half a million Americans have AGS.

entry.title: How brain remodeling during adolescence shapes memory
entry.summary: Scientists have long known that the human brain continues developing well beyond the teenage years, with important changes involving decision-making and emotional regulation extending into the mid- to late 20s. Now, for the first time, researchers at Albert E…

entry.title: What is alpha-gal syndrome? Know the signs, prevention of emerging condition
entry.summary: There’s a type of food allergy you might not expect because it doesn’t come from foods like peanuts, dairy, wheat or soy -- it actually comes from tick bites.

entry.title: Bad News: All This Summer Sun Likely Won't Fix Your Vitamin D Levels, Study Finds
entry.summary: Summer just feels like a healthier season – plenty of daylight after work to play soccer or picnic in the park, beach days, and a free boost of vitamin D from all that extra sunshine.

entry.title: I’m a personal trainer for seniors — everyone after 50 should be doing these 5 bodyweight moves to improve strength and mobility
entry.summary: Here are the exercises to add to your routine

entry.title: Rabid bat found in Seattle near University of Washington
entry.summary: Anyone who may have come in contact with the bat should call the health department and pets who may have had contact should be taken to a veterinarian.

entry.title: Children of Holocaust survivors face higher schizophrenia risk, Israeli study finds
entry.summary: It’s known that the adult children of many Holocaust survivors suffer from trauma as a result of growing up with them and hearing about their horrific experiences, or when the parents refuse to share

entry.title: Rabid horse costing Montgomery County thousands of dollars in life-saving vaccines
entry.summary: Lab results are back for a horse with rabies in Fultonville. The Montgomery County Health Department shared that it had a raccoon strain of rabies.

entry.title: Mediterranean diet may activate tiny proteins that protect the heart and brain
entry.summary: The Mediterranean diet may influence aging through tiny proteins produced inside the cell’s mitochondria. Older adults who followed the diet most closely had higher levels of humanin and SHMOOSE, which have been linked to heart and brain protection. Olive oil…

entry.title: Golden Retriever Helps Toddler Run a Successful Lemonade Stand
entry.summary: The Instagram account @hudsonbegood recently shared a video of a toddler and Golden Retriever puppy Honey Bea trying their luck at a neighborhood lemonade st...

entry.title: Map: ‘Explosive diarrhea’ parasite outbreak by state
entry.summary: More than 30 states have reported cases of the "explosive diarrhea" parasite cyclospora.

entry.title: Roseville ER doctor suffers stroke on way to work, saved by his own team
entry.summary: An emergency room physician who has spent years treating stroke patients suddenly found himself on the other side of the hospital bed after suffering a stroke on his way to work in Roseville.

entry.title: Rabid bat found near UW campus in Seattle ; public urged to seek care after any contact
entry.summary: A bat found on a busy sidewalk near the University of Washington has tested positive for rabies.

entry.title: There are more cases of Cyclosporiasis that have been reported in Mississippi.
entry.summary: Cyclospora infection has been reported in Mississippi, and health officials say it’s often linked to contaminated fresh produce.

entry.title: How Octopuses Hacked Their Ribosome To Become Smart
entry.summary: A fascinating aspect in evolutionary biology is that of convergent evolution — whereby similar structures and functions evolve independently from each other. The highly advanced nervous syste…

entry.title: Cardiologists Wish You Would Stop Doing These Things for High Blood Pressure
entry.summary: Cardiologists share 3 habits to avoid plus expert-backed steps for healthier blood pressure readings.

entry.title: The One Morning Habit Doctors Say Helps You Live Longer
entry.summary: Taking a short walk outdoors each morning may be one of the best ways to add years to your life.

entry.title: New study links ADHD symptoms in boys and addictive social media use
entry.summary: Parents should monitor patterns of social media use, not just total screen time, findings suggest.

entry.title: Two animals test positive for rabies in South Carolina counties
entry.summary: The South Carolina Department of Public Health has confirmed two animals from different counties have tested positive for rabies.

entry.title: Health officials warn of measles exposures at Tempe Marketplace, Surprise Walmart
entry.summary: Health officials say seven Maricopa County residents with measles may have exposed shoppers at Tempe Marketplace and a Surprise Walmart.

entry.title: Ritzy SoCal city rocked by rare cancer outbreak suspends use of chemical landscaping products
entry.summary: A wealthy Southern California community with a disturbing spike in rare cancer cases among children is taking action.

entry.title: ECT Reprograms Adult Neurons into a Youthful State
entry.summary: A new study unmasks Cyclin B-driven nuclear reprogramming and cellular dematuration as the mechanism of ECT.

entry.title: Drug-Resistant Gonorrhea Spreading Inside Europe, Health Officials Warn
entry.summary: Drug-resistant gonorrhea is now spreading inside Europe, ECDC reports, with clusters in France and Britain and patients who never traveled abroad.

entry.title: Golden Retriever Wearing A Cone 'Breaks Into' Woman’s Home And Demands Affection
entry.summary: “He was able to push the door open."

entry.title: Does Coffee Count Toward Your Daily Water Intake? Here’s What Experts Say
entry.summary: Coffee can count toward daily water intake for most adults, but experts say plain water matters most and caffeine limits still apply.

entry.title: Brain implant lets a paralyzed man feed himself and pet his dog
entry.summary: A brain implant let a paralyzed man feed himself and drink — his arm strength rose 86% in 35 weeks.

entry.title: What people look at most reflects their brains' specialization
entry.summary: While people explore the environment around them, their eyes constantly move between different objects, faces and other specific segments of a visual scene. This dynamic process allows them to prioritize visual information relevant to a task at hand or that t…

entry.title: New Research Reveals How Humans Could Live Up to 156 Years
entry.summary: A team of researchers has unveiled a new mathematical model suggesting humans could theoretically live up to 156 years.

entry.title: 7 things a morning headache can reveal about your health
entry.summary: Sometimes, head pain early in the day means you just slept wrong. Other times, it could be a sign of a health condition.

entry.title: The leading risk factor for cancer – and it’s not smoking, alcohol or the sun
entry.summary: The factor most associated with cancer is unavoidable, and a condition that we will all experience

entry.title: The spice found to control blood sugar and protect brain cells
entry.summary: Studies suggest ginger has many health benefits, from managing pain to reducing inflammation
